title: Support for writing to inout in fork after timing
body:
This PR fixes lifetimes of static tasks arguments which allows to write to `inout` variables after a timing control. It is done by making the variable static when the tasks has any timing control node.

It also introduces a change in `V3Begin`, where aside from moving the static variable outside of a task, we add a port variable and `Assign`s to move the value from/to arguments based on the direction of the argument.

### Example 

~~~systemverilog
  task t1(output int x);
    fork
      x = #1 1;
    join_none
  endtask
~~~

Before, `x` variable was automatic and this code would result in unsupported error. Now, during `V3LinkParse` `x` is marked as static and in `V3Begin` it is split into two - `__Vstatic_x` that is moved outside the function and `x` port variable to accept the argument. Because the argument has `output` direction a `Assign` from `__Vstatic_x` to `x` is added at the end of `t1` body. If the direction was `input`, an `Assign` from `x` to `__Vstatic_x` would be added at the beginning of the task, and if it was `inout` both would be added.
